name: Maine (United States)
description: A field of blue (the same shade as the canton of the U.S. flag) bearing in the centre the coat of arms of Maine: a shield with a tall pine tree and a reclining moose against a green field, supported by a farmer with a scythe and a sailor leaning on an anchor; above the shield, the North Star and the Latin motto 'DIRIGO'; below, a ribbon with the word 'MAINE'.
history: Adopted by an Act of the Maine Legislature on 24 February 1909, replacing an earlier 1901 'pine tree and star' flag (a buff field with a green pine tree and a blue North Star at the canton). Periodic proposals to revive the simpler 1901 design have been advanced — most recently a 2024 ballot question — but as of 2026 the 1909 coat-of-arms flag remains the official flag of Maine.

features.symbolism: central shield with a pine tree and reclining moose; supported on the dexter by a farmer with a scythe and on the sinister by a sailor with an anchor; above is a banderole reading 'DIRIGO' ('I lead') and a North Star; below, a ribbon bearing the word 'MAINE'
